dialogue.utterance: Unit 4, dispatched for a reported suicide attempt via BB gun in Richmond.
Hi, sir, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. Can you tell me what's going on today?
I shot myself with a BB gun. I'm not sure if it went in, but I'm here now.
Can you tell me a bit more about your injuries or what you're most concerned about?
What's your name?
John. My name is John.
What city are we in right now?
Henrico. I should know this, I suppose.
What's today's date?
July 11th, maybe the 12th. I'm not certain.
And finally, why did you call 911 today?
Because the pain wasn't bad, and I'm still here. I wasn't sure if I meant it, but it seemed like the only option for now.
How alert are you? Are you opening your eyes on your own?
I am. I don't feel asleep. I just... I wake up like this every day.
That's fine, John. How would you describe the injuries you've caused?
I told you. BB gun in the leg, some scratches with a knife on the arm. Not serious, probably. I just want to show something.
Would you be able to squeeze my fingers for me now? One hand at a time.
Yes. I'll try. It feels stable, or maybe strangely broken in another way.
Let's quickly check your airway. Do you feel any difficulty breathing?
No, actually. I haven't struggled to breathe. Thank you for noticing signs no one else would.
Partner, can you count the respirations and check for airway clearance now?
Respirations at 24 per minute, SpO2 at 99%.
We're going to take your pulse and blood pressure, John. Would you be comfortable with that?
Pulse is 105, Blood Pressure is 146 over 84. Skin is warm, dry, and pale.
I just need to confirm the location of your wounds. Legs and arm, correct?
Right calf from the BB. Superficial check on the left forearm. Just lines. All of it-nonsense, maybe. But it feels like a desperate attempt at something.
Have you experienced any dizziness or hypotension?
Not really. I feel a little odd, but not faint. Like maybe I just thought more than others expect me to.
Do you have any known past medical conditions you'd like to share?
Major depression. A brain injury previously. Nothing gets recognized unless obvious. But I handle things the way they come.
What medications do you currently take?
Lexapro. Trileptal. Synthroid. Imipramine. Abilify. Aripiprazole. I know I should be grateful. Maybe I am. Maybe I just don't know it yet.
Do you have any allergies?
I've had bad reactions to Topamax and haloperidol before, but that was in the past.
When was the last time you had something to eat?
Not sure. Maybe a few hours ago. I haven't felt hunger the same in a while.
When did you first notice your symptoms?
The arm and leg maybe a few minutes ago. The brain... stages it every day. I've felt like that for weeks now.
How would you rate your pain now, from 0 to 10?
Zero. Because it's not physical. I don't get to be taken seriously with this kind of pain.
John, partner and I will examine the wounds now.
Sure. Let me prove I'm real to someone, if that's what you want.
Right calf has a small puncture. Left forearm has multiple superficial cuts. No active bleeding. Skin is pale, dry, and warm.
We'll check your eyes and neck in a moment. Good with that?
Pupils are PERRLA. Midline trachea. No JVD observed.
I'll check your lungs. You okay with that?
Breath sounds are clear in all fields. No abnormal breathing sounds. Chest is symmetric and intact.
Are you comfortable with me briefly checking your abdomen for tenderness?
Abdomen is soft, not tender, and not swollen.
Let me examine your back and limbs. You good with that?
No back deformities noted. Left arm has +PMSx4. Wounds as reported.
Pulse is 105 right now. Still high, but stable. Tachycardia is present.
Respiratory rate is 24-still elevated.
Blood pressure shows 146 over 84-elevated. Indicates mild hypertension.
Glucose is 108-considered normal right now.
Oxygen saturation is 99%.
I'm going to assist with dressing these wounds now. Injuries don't bleed, but it's good to cover them.
Go ahead. They're just things I did. I don't even like to see them.
Partner has applied bandage and gauze to the wounds. Hemorrhage control achieved with direct pressure. No further bleeding observed.
Minor wounds bandaged. No changes required unless bleeding returns. Wounds at rest now.
Partner will start an IV in your arm to give fluids. It's a standard step for transport. You okay with that?
Sure. I'll just accept. I don't know why I even object. It's for the whole process.
IV started in the left extremity vein with 18-gauge needle. Normal saline of 200 ml infused per protocol 4-14. No pain medication necessary for now.
We're adhering to Protocol 4-14 for hemorrhage control. No changes are needed at this stage.
Let me check your pulse once more, John. Just to make sure things are still steady.
Pulse is 102, Blood Pressure is 126/77, respiratory rate 22. Oxygen saturation is 99%. No changes in sensation or movement observed.
No further meds or interventions deemed necessary per protocol.
On the same pain scale, how do you feel now?
Still zero. It's not something people even register as pain. But it's real for me.
We'll be going to Chippenham Medical Center for evaluation. Still on board for that, John?
Yep. I just had to try that out there. But I guess it's easier with people helping than trying alone.
We need to transfer you to the stretcher now, John. Let's make sure it's comfortable for you.
Move me. Just keep me moving forward this time. Not away from myself anymore. Just not alone.
Stretcher secured with five seatbelts. Patient in a semi-Fowler's position, comfortable for transport with good CA+Ox4.
All data will be transferred to the hospital. You're ready for the ride now.
Hospital has been notified via cell phone and is expecting us on arrival.
We're on our way to the ER now. If you just stay lying and calm, you'll be at the hospital in no time.
Transferring to Trauma ER Bed 4. Movement secured, patient aware and comfortable during the shift.
Any issues on the shift? We're clearly set, and ready for the medical team.
GCS is 15, with Eye = 4, Verbal = 5, Motor = 6.
Any history of hospitalizations or surgeries for your depression or brain injury?
No new surgeries. I cope the only way I could. Not much changed physically.

31-year-old male patient with self-inflicted wounds to the right calf and left forearm from a BB gun and a knife. Call type is stab/gunshot wound/penetrating trauma. No active bleeding at this time. IV bag with 200 ml normal saline established in the left arm per standing order. Pertinent past medical history includes major depression and traumatic brain injury.
Patient stable. Vitals at 126/77, pulse 102, respiratory 22, oxygen 99%, and glucose 108. Handoff completed with Cazzie Britton, RN.
